EST. 1976 · NO MAN'S LN, DALE ABBEY

Where the milk
churns became tea rooms.

Five generations of the Sisson family have farmed these 91 acres since 1901. In 2017 we swapped milking stools for cake stands — same barn, same family, a whole lot more butter icing.

FROM DAIRY TO TEAROOM

A farm that fed Ilkeston before it fed afternoon tea.

Sandiacre Lodge Farm has been worked by the Sisson family for five generations. You're sitting in the original cowshed, where cows were milked twice daily — hence the name. Between 1976 and 2001 it was a working dairy; today it's beef cattle, an orchard we planted in 2010, and the cake stand in front of you.

The building itself is around 150 years old, though its current shape dates to the 1950s. In 2017 the family carefully restored it into the tea rooms you're sat in now — same stone, same beams, new purpose.

1976

The dairy years begin

Sandiacre Lodge Farm starts producing milk from these very sheds, milking twice a day.

2001

Beef takes over

The farm shifts from dairy to rearing beef cattle, keeping the land working.

2010

The orchard is planted

Old-variety apple, plum, pear and damson trees go in — still harvested each year for the kitchen.

2017

The Cowshed opens

The original milking shed is sympathetically restored and reborn as a homemade-food tearoom.

Homemade cakes, daily 91 acres, one family Dine-in · Takeaway · Delivery Fresh orchard fruit since 2010 Homemade cakes, daily 91 acres, one family Dine-in · Takeaway · Delivery Fresh orchard fruit since 2010
SERVED ALL DAY

The menu, straight from the counter.

Everything's cooked in-house — from the soup to the sausage rolls. Ask a member of staff about allergies or intolerances before ordering.
